# TDEINTERLEAVE

<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_golden_manual.py; do not edit. -->

## Introduction

De-interleave one or two source tiles into an even-position destination and an
odd-position destination. This is the inverse of `TINTERLEAVE`.

## Math Interpretation

For the flattened input sequence `x`, `dst_even[i] = x[2*i]` and
`dst_odd[i] = x[2*i + 1]`. The two-source form applies the same mapping to the
logical concatenation of its sources.

## C++ Intrinsic

```cpp
template <typename DstTile, typename SrcTile>
PTO_INST void TDEINTERLEAVE(
    DstTile &dst_even, DstTile &dst_odd, SrcTile &src);
template <typename DstTile, typename SrcTile>
PTO_INST void TDEINTERLEAVE(
    DstTile &dst_even, DstTile &dst_odd, SrcTile &src0, SrcTile &src1);
```

## Constraints

- All operands have the same element type and row-major layout.
- The valid column count is even.
- LinxISA 0.57 lists signed/unsigned 8-, 16-, and 32-bit integers, `float`,
  `half`, and `bfloat16_t`.
- The 0.57 workbook marks the operation as A5-only among its listed targets.

## Examples

```cpp
TDEINTERLEAVE(even, odd, packed);
```

## Assembly Status

The 0.57 workbook defines the PTO operation but does not provide a standalone
DavinciOO PTO-AS page. The selected backend supplies its block-template
lowering.
